sentence_reviews.text: Generation-time watermarking modifies sampling or output structure to embed a statistically detectable signal associated with an AI generator.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Kirchenbauer et al. propose an LLM watermark that changes token sampling using a green list and is detectable statistically from generated text.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for text generation-time watermarking; output-structure wording is a slight generalization but within scope.

sentence_reviews.text: It can support origin claims, synthetic-data filtering, and forensic analysis without trusting a generic AI-content classifier.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The watermark paper motivates detection of machine-generated text via an embedded signal rather than a generic classifier.
sentence_reviews.notes: Origin/detection and classifier contrast are supported; forensic/synthetic-data filtering are reasonable applications but not exact claims.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Kirchenbauer et al. use green-list token selection as the watermarking mechanism.
Reviewed as synthesis across cited source pointers and, where applicable, the canonical atlas graph context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Former ambiguity resolved by narrowing the audit interpretation to synthesis support rather than requiring exact-source wording.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The watermark paper supports detecting machine-generated text via an embedded statistical signal.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supported as provenance-related detection signal, though provenance framing is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Paraphrasing, editing, translation, collusion, and distribution shift can weaken detection or create false accusations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The watermark paper discusses robustness limitations and attacks such as text modification/paraphrase and detection error tradeoffs.
sentence_reviews.notes: Editing/paraphrase and errors are supported; translation/collusion/distribution-shift broaden the limitation.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Generation-time watermarking has an `evaluates_or_constrains` dependency on Autoregressive decoding because Autoregressive decoding is modeled as evaluation, constraint, audit, or governance support.

sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning/dependency claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
